content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented stdout-tail fallback for exit-failure classification in both `ProcessBackendAdapter` and `TmuxAdapter`, so plain-text stdout exhaustion messages are now fed into `classify_exit_failure_with_output()` when `extract_stdout_error()` returns `None`. Broadened backend exhaustion matching to include plain-text `insufficient credits` wording, updated the classification docs to reflect the bounded stdout fallback, and added regression coverage in both adapter-level tests and process-backend unit tests for stdout-only exhaustion detection and bounded fallback behavior.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the existing `classify_exit_failure_with_output` call sites in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` and `src/adapters/tmux.rs` plus the exhaustion matcher/tests to confirm plain-text stdout was still discarded whenever `extract_stdout_error()` returned `None`.
2. [Completed] Patched both adapters to use a shared bounded stdout fallback helper for exit classification, preserving structured stdout errors when present while allowing stdout-only exhaustion text to participate in classification.
3. [Completed] Expanded plain-text backend exhaustion matching in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` to cover `insufficient credits` wording and updated the classification documentation to reflect the new stdout fallback contract.
4. [Completed] Added regression coverage in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs`, `tests/unit/tmux_adapter_test.rs`, and the process-backend unit test module for stdout-only `BackendExhausted` classification and bounded stdout-tail fallback behavior.
5. [Completed] Ran formatting, focused regression tests, the full `cargo test` suite, and the requested broader validation commands, recording both the passing results and the pre-existing verification blockers.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt` ran successfully, and `cargo fmt --check` passed on the final tree.
- Focused regressions passed: `cargo test stdout_plain_text_exhaustion_without_stderr_is_backend_exhausted --test unit`, `cargo test tmux_adapter_classifies_stdout_only_exhaustion_as_backend_exhausted --test unit`, and `cargo test classify_with_plain_stdout_only_exhaustion_upgrades_to_backend_exhausted --lib`.
- `cargo test` passed on the final tree: lib, integration, unit, and doc-test suites completed successfully, including the new stdout-only exhaustion coverage.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` failed due pre-existing unrelated lints outside this change set, including dead code in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, large-enum / await-holding-lock findings in `src/cli/project.rs`, a collapsible match in `src/cli/run.rs`, several `too_many_arguments` findings in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, and additional pre-existing clippy violations in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs`.
- `nix build` was launched and advanced to building `/nix/store/c2dasrf0qx100jgqiq43fgqs28fdlqd6-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but no completion or failure result was emitted before the monitoring window ended.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing unrelated lints outside this change set, including `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/cli/run.rs`, `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs`.
- `nix build` was started and reached the derivation build phase for `/nix/store/c2dasrf0qx100jgqiq43fgqs28fdlqd6-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but it did not finish within the monitoring window, so the repository’s authoritative verification gate remains unconfirmed.
- The worktree was already dirty in unrelated `.ralph-burning/projects/gsp-1/` state files, and those changes were left untouched.
